opp.ais_why:
• This is a text-based RFP with no recorded calls, so all evidence comes from the Salesforce record. The strongest written signal is that Zycus has flagged AI as Very Critical for SaskTel, paired with a Tier 2 "Info Assist (AI Insights)" rating for what they want from AI in the RFP.
• Read together, those two fields say SaskTel's procurement team self-rated AI as a Very Critical evaluation criterion and explicitly wants AI Insights baked into the S2P scope. That self-authored RFP positioning is meaningful buyer-side AI pull, even without a verbatim quote, and it's enough to lift this off the resistant floor.
• What caps the score: the AI tier is Insights, not Agentic or Autonomous; no demo, POC, or champion has been confirmed because there are no Avoma calls; and a text RFP can mask whether the Very Critical rating is genuine excitement or procurement-checklist hygiene. Confidence stays Low until live engagement validates.

items.quote: Only procurement contacts are mapped; the finance approver implied by the year-end books gate is unnamed.
No confirmation whether Zycus advanced past the April down-selection or how it scored.
No cost, savings, or cycle-time figures captured anywhere.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (14 April 2026, Jeffrey Buzila: closing year-end books)
SF AIS_Why__c (text RFP, no quantified case)
SF Opportunity custom fields (no metrics populated)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI is documented in any Salesforce field, activity, or call. The RFP (submitted 11 March 2026) covers a full S2P scope, which implies a modernization driver, but no metric (cost savings, cycle-time reduction, error rate, headcount efficiency) has been captured. Jeffrey Buzila's 14 April 2026 note about closing year-end books implies a finance gate and budget dependency, yet with no quantified value there is nothing to justify the $358k investment to a CFO or to separate Zycus from the four shortlisted rivals. The move is to reconstruct the business case from the RFP and quantify the pain into a CFO-ready savings model on re-engagement.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Adriel Picard: Decision Maker; Jeffrey Buzila: no role)
SF Next_Step__c (14 April email; 6 June no response to invites)
Avoma introductory call 2025-01-28 (small talk only, no business content)
champion.narrative: No champion is identified. Adriel Picard (Director of Procurement, Decision Maker) owns the RFP process but shows no advocacy: no internal intel shared, no introduction to a budget owner, no coaching on how to win. Jeffrey Buzila sent one delay email on 14 April 2026 and has not responded to follow-ups, Horizon, or Calgary Stampede invitations in ~79 days, indicating passive or cooling engagement. The relationship is transactional (RFP issued, questions answered, submission received), and the only recorded live call (28 January 2025) was social small talk with no business content. The move is to test whether Adriel or Jeffrey will advocate, and if not, to develop a new higher-access contact.

sources: SF Competitors__c (Coupa; GEP Worldwide; JAGGAER; SAP Ariba)
SF Task 2026-04-13 (Ariba portal used to run the RFP)
SF AIS_Why__c + Products__c (Merlin/Agentic AI as the wedge)
competition.narrative: SaskTel is running a competitive RFP with four named rivals: Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba (source: SF Competitors__c). None is confirmed as the incumbent or the buyer's preferred vendor; SaskTel merely uses the SAP Ariba supplier portal to administer the RFP, which is not the same as SAP Ariba being the current S2P provider. Competitive intelligence is otherwise zero: no scoring, no rank, no stated buyer preference on any call or field, because no vendor presentation has happened. Zycus's differentiation is the Merlin and Agentic AI scope against SaskTel's Very Critical AI criterion, but that narrative has not yet been carried into a live session. The move is to confirm shortlist status, request scoring feedback, and activate the AI story in the presentation.

sources: SF Products__c (full S2P + Merlin/Agentic AI scope)
SF AIS_Why__c (AI Very Critical)
SF Opportunity custom fields (no pain field populated)
identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer on any call or in any field. The RFP scope (iContract, iManage, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest, iRisk, plus Merlin and Agentic AI) implies SaskTel intends to modernize or consolidate its source-to-pay stack, and the Very Critical AI rating suggests appetite for AI-driven procurement intelligence, but the pain owner, severity, and compelling event are all unknown. The 79-day silence after evaluation suggests the driver may not be urgent or the owner may lack authority to force a decision. The move is to reconstruct the pain on re-engagement: what problem drives the RFP, whose team and budget it hits, and what happens if it is not solved in 2026.

sources: SF Task 2026-04-13 (checked the Ariba portal for down-selection updates)
SF Opportunity custom fields (no paper-process field populated)
SF Next_Step__c (no contracting/legal discussion)
paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are visible: MSA/SOW requirements, legal review, InfoSec, and procurement channel are all undocumented, and no partner or systems integrator has been named. SaskTel runs its sourcing through the SAP Ariba supplier portal (the platform Zycus checked for down-selection updates on 13 April 2026), which signals a formal procurement channel but says nothing about the downstream legal and security gates. With 63 days to the 4 September close and no commercial conversation started, the paper process is a hard blocker, since enterprise MSA/SOW, redlines, and InfoSec typically run 8 to 12 weeks. The move is to surface the contracting and security steps as soon as the buyer re-engages.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Adriel Picard: Director of Procurement, Decision Maker; Jeffrey Buzila: no role)
SF Next_Step__c (14 April 2026: year-end books)
SF Opportunity custom fields (no EB/sponsor field populated)
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified or engaged after 520 days. The most senior mapped contact is Adriel Picard, Director of Procurement and the primary Decision Maker on the contact roles, but a procurement director in a telecom of SaskTel's size rarely owns final budget sign-off on a multi-year S2P platform. Jeffrey Buzila's 14 April 2026 reference to closing year-end books points to a finance approval gate whose owner (a CFO or VP Finance) has never been named or contacted. The move is to ask Adriel to name and introduce the budget owner, and to map the finance approver before any commercial step.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (2026-02-12 RFP calendar; 2026-04-14 delay note)
SF Task 2026-04-15 (down-selection still taking place)
SF field history (StageName Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted 24 Nov 2025)
decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal, calendar-driven public-style RFP: issued 11 February 2026, questions due 18 February, SaskTel answers 25 February, submissions 11 March, shortlisting week of 6 April, supplier presentations week of 20 April. That process map is clear and well documented, but SaskTel has slipped its own timeline: the presentation window passed with no Zycus interview, and Jeffrey Buzila attributed the delay to year-end book-closing on 14 April 2026. The approval chain past procurement (who signs, in what order) is not documented. The move is to reconfirm where SaskTel is in its own stated process and secure the vendor-presentation slot.

sources: SF AIS_Why__c (AI Very Critical, Tier 2 Info Assist)
SF Products__c (full S2P + Merlin/Agentic AI scope)
SF Next_Step__c (RFP calendar and evaluation stages)
decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation is a formal, structured RFP, so the criteria live in the RFP document itself and in Zycus's own AI positioning rather than in a discovery call. Salesforce records AI as Very Critical for SaskTel with a Tier 2 Info Assist (AI Insights) rating, indicating AI-driven procurement intelligence is a self-authored, weighted evaluation criterion. Beyond that written signal, the specific scoring weights, must-have functional requirements, and how SaskTel is ranking the four vendors are unknown because no vendor presentation or feedback session has occurred. The move is to request the scoring rubric and criteria weighting when confirming shortlist status.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Thin signals pull the read to 43.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.
blocks.title: Deal momentum

bullets.text: Live next steps with named dates

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: SaskTel is running a four-vendor S2P RFP against Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba (SF Competitors__c).

bullets.text: No economic buyer or finance approver mapped after 520 days; only procurement is engaged.
Buyer missed its own week-of-20-April presentation window and has been silent 79 days; the 4 September close is not credible.
Single procurement thread with no champion; high risk of a silent loss at down-selection with no visibility into scoring.
No pricing, ROI, or budget-owner confirmation to justify $358k; year-end books cited as a gate.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.evidence: Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 34 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 43 is carried by competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15). Offsetting it: preference strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (moving forward): Next Step carries 7 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 18 is anchored on the stage win (43), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 34. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 7 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

deal_movement.summary: Momentum stopped after RFP submission on 11 March 2026. The only recent activity is rep-side: owner transfer from Karson Keogh to Mike Flowers on 13 April 2026, a close-date push to 4 September on 4 May 2026, and Horizon/Stampede invitations the buyer ignored. Last real buyer touch was Jeffrey Buzila's 14-15 April delay email; no buyer-facing progress in ~79 days.

reasons.detail: SaskTel ($357,898, forecast Best Case, stage Shortlisted) has logged zero activity for 79 days. Mike Flowers' 2026-07-02 next-step note confirms Jeff Buzila says the team is still tallying RFP scores, and SaskTel has not responded to either the Horizon (Sept) or Calgary Stampede invites we sent. With no champion identified and no visibility into Zycus's rank in the four-vendor S2P RFP (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba), a deal carried in Best Case risks a silent loss at down-selection and an inflated forecast.
reasons.metric: 79 days since last activity on a $357,898 Best-Case forecast
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Mike Flowers why a $358k Best-Case deal has had zero activity for 79 days, whether SaskTel has actually shortlisted us, and why it stays in Best Case with no champion and no scoring feedback.
reasons.summary: $358k Best-Case SaskTel forecast stalled 79 days; buyer ignoring our invites

No response to Horizon or Calgary stampede invites sent out.' days_since_last_activity=79; forecast_category Best Case; amount 357898.

champion_strength.summary: No champion identified. Adriel Picard owns the RFP but shows no advocacy, internal intel sharing, or introductions to a budget owner, and Jeffrey Buzila has gone quiet for ~79 days after a single delay email. The relationship is transactional and cooling, and the sole recorded call (28 January 2025) was social small talk with no business content. Without a champion, the deal is at high risk of a silent loss at down-selection.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cold. Last buyer touch was 14-15 April 2026; SaskTel ignored follow-ups plus Horizon and Calgary Stampede invitations, and no buyer-facing event has occurred in ~79 days.

north_star_verdict.math: Deal created 29 Jan 2025 (520 days old), qualified 30 Jan 2025. RFP submitted 11 Mar 2026; entered Shortlisted 24 Nov 2025. Buyer's own RFP calendar set shortlisting for week of 6 April and supplier presentations for week of 20 April 2026, both of which passed with no confirmation to Zycus. Last buyer touch 14-15 April 2026; ~79 days of silence to today (3 Jul 2026). Close date 4 Sep 2026 is 63 days out. Closing by then requires shortlist confirmation, a vendor presentation, EB mapping, pricing, negotiation, MSA/SOW, legal, and InfoSec in ~9 weeks from a standing start. Current pace is effectively stalled; required pace is not credible without immediate re-engagement.
evidence: Last buyer touch 14-15 April 2026: Jeffrey Buzila emailed that SaskTel is closing year-end books and the down-selection would push to end of April, then went silent for ~79 days. (Source: SF Task 2026-04-15, SF Next_Step__c)
Buyer's RFP calendar set supplier presentations for the week of 20 April 2026; that window passed with no scheduled Zycus interview, reschedule, or explanation. (Source: SF Next_Step__c 2026-02-12)

6 April 2026 rep note: Mike Flowers recorded Jeffrey referencing the team still tallying scores, and no response to Horizon or Calgary Stampede invitations, unconfirmed by the buyer directly. (Source: SF Next_Step__c)

No economic buyer identified or engaged after 520 days; only procurement contacts are Adriel Picard (Director of Procurement, Decision Maker) and Jeffrey Buzila (no title, no role). (Source: SF OpportunityContactRole)
No pricing, commercial, or budget conversation is documented in any Salesforce field, activity, or call. (Source: SF Q1/Q3 fields, SF Tasks)
Best Case forecast with no EB, no champion, no buyer engagement in 79 days, and 63 days to close is not defensible; honest category is Pipeline. (Source: reconciled read)
north_star_verdict.headline: 79-day buyer silence after the missed April interview window; a 4 September close is indefensible with no economic buyer, no champion, and no commercial engagement.

flags: Break the 79-day silence: get the owner a direct reply from Adriel or Jeffrey on shortlist status before the September close becomes unreachable.
Break single-thread: both known contacts are procurement; identify and reach the finance/budget owner implied by the year-end books gate.
Activate the AI wedge: put Merlin and Agentic AI Insights in front of SaskTel against the Very Critical AI criterion, since no demo has yet carried that story.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a Shortlisted, sizeable, four-vendor RFP that has gone quiet for 79 days with a single procurement thread and no budget owner. The highest-leverage levers are breaking the silence, breaking single-thread, and getting the AI wedge in front of SaskTel before selection.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer statement of preference for Zycus or any rival on any call or field; the only recorded call (28 Jan 2025) was social small talk.

competitive_position.summary: SaskTel is running a four-vendor S2P RFP against Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba (SF Competitors__c). No competitor is confirmed incumbent or buyer-preferred: SaskTel administers the RFP through the SAP Ariba supplier portal, which is a sourcing platform, not proof SAP Ariba is the current provider. There is zero visibility into scoring or Zycus's rank because no vendor presentation or feedback has occurred, so all four sit as active, roughly equal, medium threats. The single most useful move is to confirm shortlist status and pull scoring feedback; the wedge is Merlin/Agentic AI against SaskTel's self-rated Very Critical AI criterion.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Listed as a shortlisted competitor on the SaskTel S2P RFP; no buyer preference or pricing intel on record.
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Merlin agentic sourcing and negotiation intelligence against Coupa on SaskTel's Very Critical AI criterion in the vendor presentation.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: Listed as a shortlisted competitor on the SaskTel S2P RFP; no buyer preference or pricing intel on record.
competitors.how_we_win: Position productized S2P plus Merlin over GEP's consulting-led model, showing faster time-to-value for a telecom procurement team.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: Listed as a shortlisted competitor on the SaskTel S2P RFP; no buyer preference or pricing intel on record.
competitors.how_we_win: Match suite breadth on the RFP checklist, then win on the Agentic AI Insights tier SaskTel rated Very Critical.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Listed as a shortlisted competitor; SaskTel administers the RFP via the Ariba supplier portal, which is the sourcing platform, not confirmed incumbency.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with modern AI procurement intelligence and lower total cost versus a heavier SAP footprint; do not concede incumbency that evidence does not confirm.

items.deliverable: Shortlist decision and vendor-presentation invitation SaskTel owes Zycus after down-selection
items.grounding_quote: We are plugging away at evaluations, but it is going to be some time (closer to end of April) before we are done and looking at the shortlisting phase.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: SaskTel self-rated AI as Very Critical at the Info Assist (AI Insights) tier and priced Merlin plus Agentic AI into scope, so the appetite is real and written, but Zycus has never activated the agentic narrative live because no demo or presentation has occurred. The AI story is well matched to the criterion but under-delivered in engagement.

items.quote: AI flagged Very Critical for SaskTel, paired with a Tier 2 Info Assist (AI Insights) rating.
RFP scope spans iContract, iManage, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest, iRisk plus Merlin and Agentic AI.
The AI tier requested is Insights, not Agentic or Autonomous; whether the Very Critical rating is genuine excitement or checklist hygiene is unvalidated without a call.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: SaskTel self-rated AI as a Very Critical evaluation criterion at the Info Assist (AI Insights) tier, which aligns to Zycus's Merlin scope; the full S2P functional fit is presumed strong from the priced scope but unvalidated because no demo or presentation has occurred.

• Read together, those two fields say SaskTel's procurement team self-rated AI as a Very Critical evaluation criterion and explicitly wants AI Insights baked into the S2P scope. That self-authored RFP positioning is meaningful buyer-side AI pull, even without a verbatim quote, and it's enough to lift this off the resistant floor.
• What caps the score: the AI tier is Insights, not Agentic or Autonomous; no demo, POC, or champion has been confirmed because there are no Avoma calls; and a text RFP can mask whether the Very Critical rating is genuine excitement or procurement-checklist hygiene. Confidence stays Low until live engagement validates.
